rulings.Case Title: DEVIN STEELE et al VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, October 28, 2013, Line 2, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion To Dismiss. The motion is continued to November 18, 2013, to give plaintiff's counsel a chance to obtain a signed release from his clients and dismiss this case pursuant to the parties' settlement. If that does not occur, then the Court will dismiss the action. The arbitration did not toll the 5-year period. See CCP 1141.17. The impracticability tolling provision does not apply because plaintiff has not been diligent. See Sanchez v. City of Los Angeles (2003) 109 Cal.App.4th 1262. Plaintiff fails to establish estoppel and the Dept. 610 rulings are irrelevant because that department did not hear a motion based on the 5-year period. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: STEFAN GIUSTINO VS. CARLOS GUSTAVO BONO ROVIROSA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Julia Bono'S Motion To Set Aside Default Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, October 28, 2013, Line 3, Defendant Julia Bono's Motion To Set Aside Default Judgment. Granted. Mexico is a party to the Hague Service Convention, and therefore, Plaintiff was required to serve Defendant, a resident of Mexico, pursuant to methods of service the Hague Service Convention authorizes. The Hague Service Convention does not authorize personal service. Mexico objected to service by mail in Article V of its declarations made at the moment of accession (1999). (See also In re Alyssa F. ((2003) 112 Cal.App.4th 846, 854, "service of process by ordinary mail does not perfect service in Mexico.") Failure to comply with Hague procedures voids service, even if it was made in compliance with California law and even if the defendant has actual notice of the suit. (Kott v. Superior Ct. (1996) 45 Cal.App.4th 1126, 1136.) Therefore, service was not properly effectuated on Defendant and the judgment entered against her is void for lack of personal jurisdiction. The default and default judgment are set aside pursuant to CCP § 473(d). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: CROWN CASTLE NG WEST INC. VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Writ Of Admin Mandamus
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, October 28, 2013, Line 6, PETITIONER CROWN CASTLE NG WEST INC.'s Motion For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus. Petitioner Crown Castle NG West Inc. ("CC") petition for writ of administrative mandate is denied. CC's request for judicial notice is granted. The Court finds that the City and County of San Francisco Board of Appeals' ("Board") decision to revoke CC's permit to install a wireless facility is supported by substantial evidence in the Administrative Record. The Board did not abuse its discretion in finding that CC's permit to install a wireless facility did not satisfy the "Planning Protected Location Compatibility Standard" and "Zoning Protected Location Compatibility Standard" under Article 25 of the San Francisco Public Works Code. CC has not shown that sufficient evidence in the Administrative Record supports its' argument that Article 25 exceeds the City's legal powers in violation of Public Utilities Code. rulings.Case Title: STEPHEN M. WILSON et al VS. BOARD OF ADMINISTRATION OF THE CALIFORNIA PUBLIC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Adminstrative Mandamus
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, October 28, 2013, Line 8, PETITIONERs STEPHEN WILSON, WENDY WILSON, DAVID WILSON, PETER WILSON's Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Adminstrative Mandamus. Denied. All of Respondent's findings were supported by the weight of the evidence. The statutes governing beneficiary designations should be "construed to effectuate the employee's intent as to whom his beneficiary should be." (Coughlin v. Board of Administration (1984) 152 Cal.App.3d 70, 72; see also Watenpaugh v. State Teachers Retirement ((1959) 51 Cal.2d 675.).) The Beneficiary Designation executed by Dr. Wilson indicates his intent for Mr. Konou to be the beneficiary of his CalPERS death benefits. Although the Beneficiary Designation was statutorily revoked, it remains valid evidence of Dr. Wilson's intent and serves as a written modification of the 2006 Domestic Partnership Agreement under Sections 14 and 19.1. Petitioners have not met their burden of showing by clear and convincing evidence that Mr. Konou knowingly and intelligently waived his right to the CalPERS death benefits, as required by Hittle v. Santa Barbara County Employees Retirement Association. ((1985) 39 Cal.3d 374.) The evidence shows that once Mr. Konou was fully apprised of the financial information pertaining to the benefits, and therefore was in a position to knowingly and intelligently waive his right, he chose not to do so. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: PHILADELPHIA INDEMNITY INSURANCE COMPANY VS. RICHARD GREENLEY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, October 28, 2013, Line 18, CROSS DEFENDANT PHILADELPHIA INDEMNITY INSURANCE COMPANY's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Denied. Philadelphia Indemnity Insurance Company ("PIIC") has met its initial threshold burden to show that the 3rd through 6th causes of action arose from the defendant's free speech or petition activity, and Mr. Huwyler has met his shifting burden of showing the probability of prevailing on the merits. (CCP § 425.16(a); Haight Ashbury Free Clinics, Inc. v. Happening House Ventures (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1539, 1547.) The Policy does not unequivocally exclude Plaintiff as an insured. (Birkhimer Decl., Exhibit H.) CIIP's own policy specifically provided a waiver of the right to recovery from Huwyler. (Birkhimer Decl., Exhibit E, § C10, p. 3.) Despite this Waiver, PIIC sued Huwyler for subrogation. To the extent that CIIP cites to specific portions of the Policy by page number, CIIP has not presented a bates stamped copy of the Policy, and therefore the argument is not supported by the evidence cited. (See, e.g., Points & Auth., p. 10:5; Najemy Decl., Exhibit H.) The objections are overruled. rulings.Case Title: MEL SIMPSON et al VS. LOWE'S COMPANIES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings On The Claims Asserted By Pltf Derrick Crumpler
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, October 28, 2013, Line 13, DEFENDANTs GINA MEACHAM, JOHN DURSO, LOWE'S HIW, INC.'s Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings On The Claims Asserted By Plaintiff Derrick Crumpler. The motion is granted without leave to amend. Plaintiff's FEHA claims are time barred under California Gov't Code § 12960(d). Plaintiff cannot base his claims on Defendant contesting his unemployment benefits application because that conduct does not constitute an adverse employment action. CCP 335.1 bars Plaintiff's common law claims. rulings.Case Title: HELEN MICKIEWICZ VS. OPERA PLAZA MASTERS ASSOCIATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Discovery Responses
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, October 28, 2013, Line 19, PLAINTIFF HELEN MICKIEWICZ's Motion To Compel Discovery Responses. Granted, in part, and denied, in part. Defendant's objections to the discovery requests are sustained, and within 30 days, Defendant shall serve further verified responses and produce responsive documents to the following limited discovery: (1) Defendant shall search the minutes of the meetings of the Opera Plaza Master Owners for a period of 10 years prior to the date of the subject accident to locate and produce all entries which in any way relate or refer to the design, maintenance, modification, repair, incidents, or injuries occurring on the inner courtyard stairs of Opera Plaza; or provide a verified response that no such minutes exist; (2) Defendant shall produce all documents in its possession reflecting or referencing a safety related complaint/concern from any source pertaining to the inner courtyard stairs of Opera Plaza for a period of three years prior to the date of the subject accident, or provide a verified response that no such documents exist; (3) Defendant shall provide the names and addresses of the designer and contractor of the courtyard stairs of Opera Plaza from 30 years ago, if known, and the same for any designer or contractor who modified the courtyard stairs of Opera Plaza; (4) Defendant shall provide the name and address of the entity/company providing security services in the nature of monitoring the security videotape facilities at Opera Plaza for a period of three years prior to the date of the subject accident. Denied as to Plaintiff's motion to compel a further response to her Request for Admissions and Form Interrogatory 17.1. Defendant has responded fully to each requested Admission and its related interrogatory. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: DIANE GREEN VS. JOYCE REBHUN*****CASE TRANSFERRED TO LOS ANGELES COUNTY*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Transfer Action
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, October 28, 2013, Line 15, DEFENDANT JOYCE REBHUN's Motion To Transfer Action. Granted. (CCP §§ 395(a), 397(a).) The contract was entered into in Los Angeles County. " 'The law is that a contract made by telephone is entered into at the place where the recipient of the call is at the time he accepts the offer. [Citation.]" (Wilson v. Scannavino (1958) 159 Cal.App.2d 369, 371. See Weil & Brown, 3:502.) In December 2010, Plaintiff contacted Defendant by telephone seeking legal representation, and Defendant accepted. (Rehbun Decl., 3, Green Decl., 3.) Defendant, who is the only named defendant, resides in Los Angeles County. There is no evidence to support that the contract was to be performed in San Francisco County. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: JUNYING YU VS. WILLIAM BAILES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant'S Renoticing Of Notice Of Special Appearance To Move To Quash Service Of Summons; Refiling Of Motion To Quash Service Of Summons; Memorandum Of Law.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, October 28, 2013, Line 20, Defendant's Renoticing Of Notice Of Special Appearance To Move To Quash Service Of Summons; Refiling Of Motion To Quash Service Of Summons; Memorandum Of Law. This motion is off calendar as moot per the Court's ruling to deny Defendants' motion to quash on October 18, 2013. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JUNYING YU VS. WILLIAM BAILES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant'S Renoticing Of Notice Of Special Appearance To Strike The Complaint Of Plaintiff Junying Yu; Motion To Strike, Filed Simulataneously With Motion To Quash Service Of Summons; Memorandum Of Law
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, October 28, 2013, Line 20, Defendant's Renoticing Of Notice Of Special Appearance To Strike The Complaint Of Plaintiff Junying Yu; Motion To Strike, Filed Simulataneously With Motion To Quash Service Of Summons; Memorandum Of Law. Defendants' motion to strike is denied. Defendants' notice of motion does not comply with CCP §1010 and CRC 3.1110, as it does not state the grounds upon which the motion is made. The basis cited in Defendants' Points and Authorities, CCP §436(a), is not grounds to strike the entire complaint. Defendants' request for judicial notice is granted to the extent of the dates the documents were recorded and the parties to the transaction. The record does not support Defendants' argument that Plaintiff's complaint is a "sham" as contemplated by case law. (See e.g. Ricard v. Grobstein, Goldman, Stevenson, Siegel, LeVine & Mangel (1992) 6 Cal.App.4th 157, 162.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
